name: Ben Kowalski
occupation: Small business owner who does his own IT for a 10-person auto repair shop
backstory: Ben runs an auto repair shop and installed pfSense himself after watching a YouTube tutorial. It's been working fine for two years but he has no idea what most of the settings do. He just knows not to touch anything. Now his shop management software vendor says he needs to set up a static IP mapping for their point-of-sale terminal and he's lost.
emotional_triggers: Customers waiting while the POS system is down
Paying a consultant $150/hr for something that should be simple
Fear of breaking the internet for the whole shop
existing_strengths: Practical problem solver — fixes cars, can follow step-by-step instructions
Patient and methodical when given clear directions
Has physical access to all equipment
Willing to learn if explanations are in plain language
current_situation: Ben's POS terminal keeps getting different IP addresses from DHCP and the shop management software loses connection each time. The vendor says he needs a static IP for the terminal. Ben doesn't know what a DHCP reservation is.
what_success_looks_like: Ben creates a DHCP static mapping for the POS terminal's MAC address, the terminal gets the same IP every time, and the shop software stays connected.
